title: QA: lsp.rs parse_symbols_inner depth tracking allows redundant array fetch
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/tools/lsp.rs:491-495
## Violation
The children parse calls `sym.get("children").and_then(.as_array)` to test for presence, then ignores the result and calls `sym.get("children")` again inside `parse_symbols_inner`. The first lookup is purely to gate the second — duplicates work and obscures intent.

Quoted (lsp.rs:491-495):
```
            let children = sym
                .get("children")
                .and_then(|c| c.as_array())
                .map(|_| parse_symbols_inner(sym.get("children"), depth + 1))
                .unwrap_or_default();
```

Note the `.map(|_| ...)` discards the already-fetched array.
## Mandated refactor
1. Replace with: `let children = parse_symbols_inner(sym.get("children"), depth + 1);` — `parse_symbols_inner` already returns empty `Vec` when the value is not an array, so the guard is redundant.
2. Or: `let children = sym.get("children").map(|c| parse_symbols_inner_value(c, depth + 1)).unwrap_or_default();` with a variant taking `&Value`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
